title: Meeting Jack Bogle
content: I had the privilege of working with Jack for years. What struck me wasn't just his brilliance - it was his consistency. The same principles he talked about publicly, he lived privately. He'd walk the halls, know people's names, ask about their families. He genuinely believed every dollar we saved investors was a victory. He used to say, 'We're not in the business of making money for ourselves.' And he meant it. The day he passed, I think we all felt we'd lost a mentor. But the mission continues...
follow_up: He got his heart transplant in 1996, the same year I joined. First thing he said coming out of anesthesia was 'Get me a pad and paper.' That was Jack.
